title: America
subtitle: 48 days · NYC to San Francisco
body:
I rode my bike from NYC to SF…

On May 25th, I set off from New York City to cycle the width of America to San Francisco for charity. Over the course of 48 days, I traversed 3,977 miles, seeing much of the country I had yet to experience. My route took me to Niagara, Canada, many of the Great Lakes, Minneapolis/St. Paul, Badlands, Yellowstone, Grand Tetons, the Nevada Desert, and Lake Tahoe.

Along the way, I was rained on, stressed by traffic and trucks, bored from looking at miles of farmland, and inspired by some of the most breathtaking views America has to offer. Our land is varied, but also overwhelmingly dedicated to agriculture.

While I saw a lot, I also felt a lot. Such a long journey will have you experiencing the full range of human emotion… from sadness, to anger and frustration, to pure elation. Most interestingly of all, I found myself at times either crying and/or laughing while feeling some of the most visceral and complex mix of emotions. Day after day, mile after mile, you whittle away at your ego until the raw emotions begin to leak out… these feelings seemingly without any single discernible reason.

While I learned a lot about myself along the way, I also learned how incredibly generous people can be… all without expecting anything in return. When pursuing such a long and arduous journey, your standard of living drops considerably and you become so much more grateful for any modicum of comfort — whether that be a shower or a bed to sleep on.

This tour had me meeting some of the most beautiful people who either provided route suggestions, money (I didn't ask for), or hosted me for a night. These interactions further enriched my experience and provided a significant boost in morale. A special thank you to those who hosted me: Diane, Amanda & Mike H., Chris, Andy & Melinda, Jim, Sam & Dave, Mike T., and Adrien & Sarah.

Lastly, I set a goal to raise $5,000 for Doctors Without Borders and ultimately raised over $10,900! A very special thank you to all who donated!

Thank you for all the love and support!
